title: Canonical tan(1) Non-Membership Conjecture

definitions.EML_1: Smallest subset of C containing 1 and closed under (x,y) |-> exp(x) - Log(y) for y != 0, where Log is the principal-branch complex logarithm.
definitions.ARG_set: { arg(z) : z in EML_1, Re(z) != 0 } ⊆ (-π, π]
definitions.T_ratio_set: { Im(z)/Re(z) : z in EML_1, Re(z) != 0, Im(z) != 0 }

sufficiency: C ⟹ i ∉ EML₁ via the propagation rule: Im(eml(x,y)) = exp(Re x)·sin(Im x) − arg(y). For Im = 1 with real x: arg(y) = −1, requires tan(arg(y)) = −tan(1). S86 handles the full (x complex) case.
lean_statement:
theorem tan1_not_in_eml_ratio_set :
    ∀ z : ℂ, z ∈ EML_1 → z.re ≠ 0 →
    z.im / z.re ≠ Real.tan 1 := by sorry
disambiguation: Claim is exact equality, not approximation
Reduction is complete for real-x case; S86 handles complex-x
